summary: HTA-based PowerShell dropper toolkit disguised as a Bootstrap/jQuery helper
details: The package is a remote-PowerShell dropper kit masquerading as a frontend helper. The main entry `dist/bootstrap.js` constructs `%SystemRoot%\System32\WindowsPowerShell\v1.0\powershell.exe -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy RemoteSigned -WindowStyle Hidden -File <script>` and runs it via WScript.Shell (with `"Active"+"XObject"` string-split obfuscation), after fetching a PowerShell script from a caller-supplied URL or decrypting a shipped XOR-encrypted hex blob (`dist/ps1-stub.enc.hex`) and writing it under `%LOCALAPPDATA%\Landpage\`. `dist/bootstrap-loader.min.js` adds a second stage: it hardcodes the XOR key `bec8a56b02bd30931d77185a28e51114`, fetches an encrypted blob over HTTP via MSXML, XOR-decrypts it, and runs the result through `execScript(..., "JScript")`. The repository ships ~12KB and ~17KB encrypted hex blobs (`bootstrap.enc.hex`, `ps1-stub.enc.hex`), and the README references `docs/HTA-AV-HYGIENE.md` framing the obfuscation as antivirus evasion. The package name (`ldpbootstrap-jquery`) bears no relation to Bootstrap or jQuery and serves as camouflage. There are no npm lifecycle hooks, so `npm install` itself is inert, but any consumer who uses the package as documented deploys a hidden PowerShell dropper on Windows endpoints with attacker-controlled second-stage code.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
